steps.title: Identify the passage's key move
steps.content: The passage reports park usage data for two cities (53.5% and 44.7%), then uses those results to test and reject a possible explanation: that the difference stems from access to nonpark amenities. The phrase “the difference in park use can't be explained by” marks this as a consider-and-reject structure.

steps.title: Match the structure to the answer
steps.content: Choice C identifies exactly this progression: a study is introduced, results are presented, a possible explanation is considered, and that explanation is rejected. A misattributes the findings to methodology, B invents an opening question, and D miscasts the data as two separate studies meant to corroborate. That matches choice C — C is correct.
